title: Honor image environment variables with exec
body:
Was reading the "env" argument twice instead of image.

~When skipping storage setup it crashes, so avoid that.~

Closes #2063

comments.body: Do we want to move this into the Exec() API call in Libpod, where we can examine the container configuration and see if a PATH was explicitly set at create time (so we can prefer that to image PATH)?

I think it needs to be done in sequence, so that variables can reuse previous variables.
1) default
2) image
3) params

But probably it can be moved into libpod, currently it is being duplicated (with "create")

It's not only PATH, but all other default variables - although currently that means TERM
Apparently images don't always have names - so try ID instead
Should the environment match the container instead of just matching the image?

I was trying to read the ENV configuration from the image, to pass it to the new container.

But it seems that Docker does copy the existing environment (including -e), from the first `run`.

This did the trick for regular podman, but haven't been able to try rootless yet.

Thanks @giuseppe !

Tested OK with rootless as well.

``` console
tc@new:~$ ./podman run -d boot2podman-docker-tinycore.bintray.io/tinycore-go:1.10.4 sleep 3600
e8ed291996c1ed69299d637151b5c25afaa45b68ecb5d1b19d7bc0b77d344f89
tc@new:~$ ./podman exec -it e8ed291996c1 sh
/ $ go version
go version go1.10.4 linux/amd64
/ $ echo $PATH
/usr/local/go/bin:/home/tc/go/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
```

Previously, it said: file not found

``` console
tc@new:~$ podman run -d boot2podman-docker-tinycore.bintray.io/tinycore-go:1.10.4 sleep 3600
6a06c845bca219da529abadce7c220d3419d4c625063f1cf510af4ca606ba48c
tc@new:~$ podman exec -it 6a06c845bca2 sh
/ $ go version
sh: go: not found
/ $ echo $PATH
/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
```

Since we have @afbjorklund and @giuseppe agreeing, I will give this a 
/lgtm
/approve
